description_long: This apartment building complex features a historic, weathered stone facade with multiple interconnected wings and a variety of rooflines, including pitched and flat sections. The main structure is adorned with arched windows, decorative cornices, and classical architectural details, suggesting an older European style. Several annexes and extensions are visible, some with covered walkways and external staircases connecting different parts of the building. The exterior shows signs of aging, with moss and discoloration on the stonework, and the windows are symmetrically arranged in rows across the facades. There are multiple entrances, including a prominent central entryway and several side doors, as well as a small round structure that could serve as a utility or service area. The overall layout suggests a large, multi-family residential building, possibly a converted historic institution or factory.
description: A large, historic stone apartment building complex with multiple wings, arched windows, and classical architectural details. The weathered facade and varied rooflines give it a distinctive, aged European appearance.
description_view_0: The main facade is visible, showcasing arched windows, decorative cornices, and a prominent central entrance with adjacent annexes.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the depth of the complex, with a clear view of the annex and a small round structure, as well as additional entrances.
description_view_2: Rear view highlights the interconnected wings, external staircases, and the symmetrical arrangement of windows across the back.
description_view_3: Opposite side view shows the length of the building, roof details, and the connection between the main structure and annexes.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are symmetrically arranged in horizontal rows across all facades, with consistent spacing and alignment, contributing to a balanced and orderly appearance.
